response:
Sec. 1  
Allergies to peanuts are immune system responses to proteins found in peanuts  
These proteins trigger reactions when they enter the body  
Living underwater does not eliminate exposure to these proteins  
People with peanut allergies can still come into contact with peanut proteins through water if the water contains traces of peanuts  
The body's immune system reacts regardless of the environment  

Sec. 2  
Underwater living does not prevent allergic reactions from occurring  
Allergic reactions depend on the presence of allergens and the immune system's response  
There is no scientific evidence that living underwater reduces the risk of peanut allergies  
The question may be based on a misunderstanding of how allergies function  
The inability to live underwater is not related to peanut allergies
